channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3037031: Convert locale.compare.inc to a service
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 28 files, +826/−328 · php +821/−328 · yml +5
  • Diff: 1629df5
  • Discussion: #3037031 · 13 contributors · 102 comments
  • Followers: 17

Summary

Drupal's locale module previously relied on a set of procedural functions in locale.compare.inc and a locale.project key-value storage service to track, build, and check the translation status of installed projects. These have been replaced by two new injectable services, LocaleProjectRepository and LocaleProjectChecker, and a typed LocaleTranslatableProject value object. The old functions and the locale.project service (LocaleProjectStorageInterface / LocaleProjectStorage) are now deprecated and will be removed in Drupal 13.0.0. Site behavior is unchanged; this affects developers who call locale translation project APIs directly.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: LocaleSource::buildSources() $projects parameter is now required (default [] removed); LocaleSource::sourceBuild() now requires a typed LocaleTranslatableProject argument; LocaleFetch and LocaleFileManager constructor dependencies changed from LocaleProjectStorageInterface to LocaleProjectRepository.
  • Deprecation: locale_translation_flush_projects(), locale_translation_build_projects(), locale_translation_project_list(), _locale_translation_prepare_project_list(), locale_translation_default_translation_server(), locale_translation_check_projects(), locale_translation_check_projects_local(), LocaleProjectStorageInterface, and LocaleProjectStorage deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0; use LocaleProjectRepository and LocaleProjectChecker instead.
  • New API: New LocaleProjectRepository service (replaces locale.project / LocaleProjectStorageInterface), new LocaleProjectChecker service, and new LocaleTranslatableProject value object added in locale module.

Technical details

The core problem was that all project-list logic lived in a single procedural include file (locale.compare.inc) with a mix of storage, building, and status-checking concerns, making it hard to inject, test, or extend. The solution splits this into three new symbols in core/modules/locale/src/:

LocaleProjectRepository is a readonly service that owns the locale.project key-value store and an in-process cache.memory cache (keyed locale_get_projects). It provides getAll(), getMultiple(), set(), deleteMultiple(), deleteAll(), and buildProjects(). The latter replaces locale_translation_build_projects(): it reads module and theme extension lists via ModuleExtensionList/ThemeExtensionList, normalises dev-version strings, fires hook_locale_translation_projects_alter, and persists LocaleTranslatableProject objects. Projects no longer in the extension list are marked disabled rather than deleted.

LocaleProjectChecker wraps the two project-checking paths: checkProjects() (replaces locale_translation_check_projects()) dispatches to a batch if remote sources are enabled, otherwise calls checkLocalProjects() (replaces locale_translation_check_projects_local()), which uses LocaleSource::sourceBuild() and saves the result via locale_translation_status_save().

LocaleTranslatableProject is a typed value object with named constructor parameters (name, type, core, version, server_pattern, info, langcode, weight, status). It replaces the bare stdClass objects that locale.compare.inc previously cast arrays into. It carries toArray() and createFromArray() for round-tripping through the key-value store.

Three existing services (LocaleSource, LocaleFetch, LocaleFileManager) previously injected LocaleProjectStorageInterface; they now inject LocaleProjectRepository directly. LocaleSource::sourceBuild() now requires a typed LocaleTranslatableProject $project instead of a bare $project object, and buildSources() now requires array $projects (the default = [] was removed). drupal_static_reset('locale_translation_project_list') now triggers a deprecation and is a no-op; drupal_static_reset('locale_translation_get_projects') was updated to call cache.memory->delete('locale_get_projects') instead of the removed locale.project->resetCache().

Upgrade

Replace deprecated procedural functions and the locale.project service as follows:

// locale_translation_flush_projects()
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectRepository::class)->deleteAll();

// locale_translation_build_projects()
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectRepository::class)->buildProjects();

// locale_translation_check_projects($projects, $langcodes)
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectChecker::class)->checkProjects($projects, $langcodes);

// locale_translation_check_projects_local($projects, $langcodes)
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectChecker::class)->checkLocalProjects($projects, $langcodes);

// \Drupal::service('locale.project')->getProjects() / getAll()
\Drupal::service(\Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectRepository::class)->getAll();

// locale_translation_project_list()
// _locale_translation_prepare_project_list()
// locale_translation_default_translation_server()
// No replacement provided; remove calls to these functions.

// drupal_static_reset('locale_translation_project_list')
// No replacement; remove the call.

If you inject LocaleProjectStorageInterface or LocaleProjectStorage as a constructor dependency, replace the type hint and service reference with LocaleProjectRepository:

// Before
public function __construct(
  protected readonly LocaleProjectStorageInterface $localeProjectStorage,
) {}

// After
public function __construct(
  protected readonly \Drupal\locale\LocaleProjectRepository $localeProjectRepository,
) {}

LocaleSource::buildSources() no longer accepts an empty default for $projects; callers that relied on buildSources() with no arguments must now pass an explicit array of project names or use LocaleProjectRepository::getAll() to obtain the full list first.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 2, 2019
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (7 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • kishor_kolekar (QED42)
  • daffie
  • hardik_patel_12 (QED42)
  • msuthars (QED42)
  • sutharsan (ronder)
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)

Collaboration

nicxvan took over a six-year-old issue in May 2025 and started from scratch because no existing patch could be applied across the 13-file conflict surface. While driving the MR to green through a long series of functional test failures, nicxvan noted a confusing overlap between LocaleTranslatableProject and the $source objects used inside LocaleSource, where a type property was being silently overwritten between the two representations. berdir pushed a set of commits directly to the MR that restored LocaleSource's handling almost exactly to HEAD, deliberately keeping the two objects separate and deferring their unification to the existing follow-up #1842380. berdir also filed #3589344 to address the enabled/disabled project status complexity that remained. claudiu.cristea, who originally opened the issue in 2019, returned for the final review round and helped shape the scope of the patch.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3525392: Add constraint for ResponseStatus Condition status code so it can be FullyValidatable
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +51/−0 · php +44 · yml +7
  • Diff: f17f0ce
  • Discussion: #3525392 · 6 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The response_status block visibility condition now has a fully validated config schema. Its status_codes values are constrained to the three HTTP codes the plugin actually supports: 200, 403, and 404. Config that was previously accepted silently at the schema layer, but contained unsupported status codes, will now produce validation errors. UI-created config is unaffected because the condition's checkbox form already restricts input to those three codes; this change closes the gap for config import, configuration actions, and programmatic edits.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: The condition.plugin.response_status config schema is now FullyValidatable; status_codes items are constrained to the Choice [200, 403, 404].

Technical details

In core/modules/system/config/schema/system.schema.yml, the condition.plugin.response_status schema type gains two additions: a top-level FullyValidatable: ~ constraint, which opts the entire type into strict typed-config validation, and a Choice constraint on the items of the status_codes sequence limiting accepted values to [200, 403, 404], matching exactly what ResponseStatus::buildConfigurationForm() exposes. Previously, the schema placed no constraint on the integers stored in status_codes, so any integer was accepted even though ResponseStatus::evaluate() only branches on those three codes. The related schema corrections to the parent condition.plugin type were handled in #3525391. A testStatusCodesValidation kernel test with a providerStatusCodesValidation data provider was added to ResponseStatusConditionTest to verify that the three valid codes pass and that an unsupported code (HTTP 418) produces the expected violation on status_codes.N.

Upgrade

If you have configuration (exported YAML or programmatically created) that sets status_codes to any integer other than 200, 403, or 404 on a response_status block visibility condition, typed-config validation will now report a violation:

The value you selected is not a valid choice.

Correct the stored config to use only the supported codes before running config import or any validation pass.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 20, 2025
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (11 months and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • irafah (Zoocha)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3552922: Add symfony/runtime dependency to core
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +13/−5 · json +9/−3 · php +4/−2
  • Discussion: #3552922 · 10 contributors · 33 comments
  • Followers: 16

Summary

Drupal core now declares symfony/runtime as a direct Composer dependency. The Symfony Runtime component provides a standardized, decoupled way to handle application bootstrapping outside of DrupalKernel, enabling bootstrap logic to be moved into swappable runtime classes. The dependency was added as a prerequisite for the upcoming Drupal CLI (#3453474) and for future front-controller improvements (#3313404). The component itself is not yet used in this change; it becomes available for those follow-on issues to build on.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Requirements: New symfony/runtime ^8.0 (pinned to ~v8.0.8 in core) added to Drupal core; requires PHP 8.4 and Composer plugin API v2, matching existing Drupal Symfony dependencies.

Technical details

The diff touches several composer configuration files. core/composer.json receives symfony/runtime ~v8.0.8 in its require block. core-recommended/composer.json receives the same package with the looser ^8.0 constraint. Because symfony/runtime ships as a Composer plugin (it registers an autoload bootstrap handler), all affected composer.json files must also list it under allow-plugins. Accordingly, the package is added with true in core/composer.json's allow-plugins and in core-recommended/composer.json's equivalent section. ComposerPluginsValidator::$allowedPlugins in core is updated to include 'symfony/runtime' => "*" so Drupal's own plugin validation does not reject it. The fake-site test fixture gains "symfony/runtime": false under allow-plugins (matching the convention that scaffold and other core plugins are explicitly disallowed there), and the accompanying test assertion in ComposerPluginsValidatorTest::providerAllowedPlugins() is updated to expect the new entry. No PHP runtime behavior changes with this commit.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 17, 2025

Key contributors

  • kingdutch
  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • ghost of drupal past
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)
  • mradcliffe (Kosada)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

Release management sequencing shaped the timeline noticeably. After initial RTBC from klausi and release-manager support from xjm, xjm paused the issue at DrupalCon Vienna 2025: the concern was that adding the dependency to a minor release without the functionality it unlocks would leave an unused package in HEAD through potentially multiple Drupal versions. The agreed approach was to wait until one of the consuming issues (#3453474 Drupal CLI or #3313404 front-controller refactor) reached RTBC, then land this dependency change and immediately rebase the consumer. larowlan later flagged the issue as blocking the Drupal CLI strategic priority, which prompted godotislate to obtain explicit release-manager approval from catch and longwave. mradcliffe rebased the MR against current main, updated the pinned version to ~v8.0.8 (the Symfony 8-era release), and confirmed ComposerIntegrationTest passed locally. The changes were ultimately merged as part of #3313404.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3313404: Use symfony/runtime for less bespoke bootstrap/compatibility with varied runtime environments
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 33 files, +378/−67 · php +322/−64 · template +35 · yml +11 · json +9/−3 · htaccess +1
  • Diff: 57bf323
  • Discussion: #3313404 · 19 contributors · 81 comments
  • Followers: 100

Summary

Drupal's front-controllers (index.php, update.php) and core CLI scripts now bootstrap through symfony/runtime. A new autoload_runtime.php glue file is generated by the scaffold plugin on every composer install, replacing the former pattern of directly requiring autoload.php, instantiating DrupalKernel, creating a Request, and calling handle/send/terminate by hand. The runtime layer creates a clean boundary between environment setup and request handling. Existing custom front-controllers using the old pattern continue to work unchanged. The practical payoff comes in follow-on issues: once this foundation is in place, environment-setup code currently buried in DrupalKernel (error handlers, site-path discovery, request globals) can be moved into the runtime and made replaceable without touching every front-controller again.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Requirements: New Composer dependency on symfony/runtime ~v7.3; symfony/runtime Composer plugin must be allowed in allow-plugins.
  • API change: DrupalKernel::__construct() first parameter widened from string to string|\Drupal\Core\AppContext; passing AppContext is the new recommended pattern from the generated front-controllers.
  • New API: New public classes DrupalRuntime (extends SymfonyRuntime, bypassing dotenv) and AppContext (readonly value object holding runtime options and getEnvironment()); new autoload_runtime.php glue file generated by the scaffold plugin; APP_RUNTIME env var to select the runtime class.

Technical details

The diff adds symfony/runtime ~v7.3 as a Drupal core dependency and introduces two new public classes. DrupalRuntime (in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Runtime/) extends SymfonyRuntime but calls GenericRuntime::__construct() directly to bypass the dotenv-handling constructor in SymfonyRuntime, since Drupal does not use dotenv. AppContext (in core/lib/Drupal/Core/) is a readonly value object holding runtime options; its getEnvironment() reads from an env-var key stored in those options and falls back to 'prod'.

The scaffold plugin gains a GenerateAutoloadRuntimeReferenceFile class that generates autoload_runtime.php in the web root (parallel to the existing autoload.php generation). This file returns a readonly anonymous class (referred to as "the glue") that on construction loads the autoloader, reads APP_RUNTIME from $_SERVER or $_ENV to select the runtime class (defaulting to DrupalRuntime), instantiates the runtime, and creates an AppContext. Calling the glue object with any application instance delegates to $this->runtime->getRunner($app)->run() and exits. The APP_RUNTIME environment variable therefore allows third parties to substitute a different runtime (such as FrankenPHP or AMPHP) without modifying any front-controller.

DrupalKernel::__construct() now accepts string|AppContext as its first parameter. When an AppContext is passed, the kernel extracts the environment string from it via getEnvironment(), preserving full backward compatibility for callers that pass a plain string. The updated front-controllers use $glue->appContext and $glue->autoloader as the constructor arguments. A test module (legacy_front_controller_test) and a functional test (LegacyFrontControllerTest) confirm that the pre-existing bootstrap pattern continues to work. The symfony/runtime Composer plugin is added to the list of plugins explicitly allowed by the scaffold plugin validator. Related: the companion dependency-declaration issue is #3552922.

Upgrade

For most sites managed through the scaffold plugin, no manual changes are needed. autoload_runtime.php is generated automatically on composer install.

Allow the symfony/runtime Composer plugin in your project's root composer.json if your project explicitly manages allow-plugins:

{
  "config": {
    "allow-plugins": {
      "symfony/runtime": true
    }
  }
}

Custom front-controllers using the old pattern still work. To migrate a custom front-controller to the new pattern:

// Old pattern:
use Drupal\Core\DrupalKernel;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Request;

$autoloader = require_once 'autoload.php';
$kernel = new DrupalKernel('prod', $autoloader);
$request = Request::createFromGlobals();
$response = $kernel->handle($request);
$response->send();
$kernel->terminate($request, $response);

// New pattern:
use Drupal\Core\DrupalKernel;

$glue = require_once 'autoload_runtime.php';
$glue(new DrupalKernel($glue->appContext, $glue->autoloader));

Code that type-hints the first argument of DrupalKernel::__construct() as string should be updated to accept string|\Drupal\Core\AppContext.

Selecting a custom runtime at runtime is possible by setting the APP_RUNTIME environment variable to the fully-qualified class name of any class implementing Symfony\Component\Runtime\RuntimeInterface. Set it in $_SERVER (via the web server) or $_ENV before autoload_runtime.php is required.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: October 4, 2022
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (3 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • bradjones1 (Not Vanilla, Inc.)
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • el7cosmos
  • kingdutch (Open Social)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • ghost of drupal past
  • shalini_jha
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • mxr576 (Pronovix)
  • znerol
  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • kim.pepper (PreviousNext)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • mradcliffe (Kosada)

Collaboration

The thread contains a substantive design debate. donquixote challenged whether the MR had clear payoff, since it added new code without yet simplifying DrupalKernel, and asked for a proof-of-concept showing the benefit. kingdutch responded with a detailed walkthrough of DrupalKernel's four conflated responsibilities and explained that the event-loop integration work in #3425210 made the problem concrete: EventLoop::run must live outside the kernel, but without a well-defined place to put it, the only option was duplicating code across all front-controllers. nicxvan questioned whether using symfony/runtime's resolver mechanism added unnecessary complexity given that FrankenPHP runtimes in the wild do not use it, and asked whether DrupalKernelRunner was redundant. kingdutch explained that the resolver is deliberately prepared for future issues (such as #2501901 for env-var-controlled environment names) and that DrupalKernelRunner was kept separate from Symfony's HttpKernelRunner to avoid silently adopting Symfony's output-buffering defaults and error-handler registration in the same change. ghost of drupal past proposed a simpler autoload template that skips the resolver entirely and passes the kernel directly. znerol flagged thread-safety concerns around putenv/getenv in multi-threaded runtimes like FrankenPHP and recommended exclusive reliance on $_SERVER. kingdutch addressed the putenv concern by noting the code is copied from SymfonyRuntime for consistency and that the console context in which it appears is not subject to threading, while pushing back on dropping $_ENV support as that would break documented Symfony Runtime behavior. catch confirmed after an in-person discussion in Vienna with kingdutch and alexpott that the approach is sound and cleared the framework manager review tag.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2486267: Attributes of a block content are applied to block itself
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +81/−6 · php +81/−6
  • Diff: 701ea27
  • Discussion: #2486267 · 17 contributors · 70 comments
  • Followers: 25

Summary

A long-standing bug in block rendering caused the #attributes from a block plugin's content render array to be incorrectly merged into the outer block wrapper element. For example, a form rendered inside a block would have its data-drupal-selector attribute, CSS classes, and other attributes moved from the <form> tag to the surrounding block <div>, breaking CSS, JavaScript selectors, AJAX behavior, and in some cases causing white-screen errors. The fix corrects BlockViewBuilder so that only #wrapper_attributes (a separate, explicitly intended key) from the content render array are promoted to the block wrapper. Regular #attributes now stay with the content element where they belong. A backward-compatibility preprocess hook is added to the Stable9 theme so that sites using it as a base theme are not immediately broken.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and front-end developers.

  • Theme change: Block content #attributes no longer bubble up to the block wrapper <div>; use #wrapper_attributes in build() to intentionally target the wrapper. Themes not based on Stable9 may need to update CSS/JS selectors.
  • API change: BlockViewBuilder::preRender() now reads #wrapper_attributes (not #attributes) from the content render array to populate the block wrapper; block plugins targeting the outer wrapper must switch to #wrapper_attributes.

Technical details

The problem originated in #2171269, which added logic to BlockViewBuilder::preRender() that moved both #attributes and #contextual_links from the block plugin's $content render array into the top-level $build array. While that was correct for #contextual_links, applying it to #attributes was too broad: any attribute a plugin set for its own content element (a <form>, a list, etc.) was silently hijacked by the block wrapper.

The fix in BlockViewBuilder::preRender() splits the two properties: #contextual_links still bubbles as before, but #attributes is no longer touched. Instead, a new convention is established: if a block plugin's build() array contains #wrapper_attributes, those are merged into $build['#attributes'] (the outer wrapper). This mirrors the existing #wrapper_attributes convention used elsewhere in Drupal's render system.

For backward compatibility, a hook_preprocess_block() implementation is added to Stable9Hooks in the stable9 theme. It copies any #attributes found in $variables['content'] into $variables['attributes'] (the wrapper) and then unsets the content key, replicating the old behavior for themes that extend Stable9. The TestHtmlBlock test plugin and its accompanying functional test (BlockHtmlTest) are updated to use wrapper_attributes through the key-value store, aligning the test fixture with the new convention.

Upgrade

Block plugins that previously relied on #attributes in their build() render array to style or identify the block wrapper element must switch to #wrapper_attributes:

// Before (broken): #attributes on the content was incorrectly merged
// into the block wrapper, not the content element.
public function build(): array {
  return [
    '#theme' => 'item_list',
    '#items' => $items,
    '#attributes' => ['class' => ['my-list']],
  ];
}

// After: use #wrapper_attributes to intentionally target the block wrapper.
public function build(): array {
  return [
    '#theme' => 'item_list',
    '#items' => $items,
    '#attributes' => ['class' => ['my-list']],   // stays on the rendered element
    '#wrapper_attributes' => ['class' => ['my-block-wrapper-class']],
  ];
}

Custom or contrib themes that do not extend Stable9 and that have CSS or JavaScript selectors depending on content #attributes appearing on the block wrapper <div> will need to audit those selectors. The Stable9 backward-compatibility hook is only active for themes using Stable9 as a base theme.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 10, 2015
  • Committed: May 9, 2026 (11 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • joelpittet
  • effulgentsia (Acquia)
  • martygraphie
  • taran2l (Lemberg Solutions)
  • johnjw59 (Fuse Interactive)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dcam (USDA-ARS)
  • tim.plunkett (Acquia)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • nod_ (Très Bien Tech)

Collaboration

dcam reopened the stalled issue by verifying the bug still existed in Drupal 11, identified that the earlier MR 1402 had silently lost the core #attributes to #wrapper_attributes switch (making it do nothing useful), hid the old branch, and restarted cleanly for the 11.x branch. dcam also provided a concrete screenshot and a white-screen crash scenario to demonstrate that the bug had real production consequences. After alexpott noted that a BC layer for Stable9 was needed before committing, dcam added Stable9Hooks::preprocessBlock() and a unit test for it. nod_ gave frontend framework manager sign-off, after which catch committed the fix.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3463868: Two #config_targets error when used on a text_format form element
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +65/−3 · php +62/−3 · yml +3
  • Diff: 712188e
  • Discussion: #3463868 · 11 contributors · 50 comments
  • Followers: 19

Summary

Configuration forms that use the #config_target property on a text_format (rich text) field threw a LogicException on submission: "Two #config_targets both target..." The bug made it impossible to wire a text_format element directly to configuration using the standard ConfigFormBase mechanism. After this fix the combination works, and the form also correctly re-displays the saved value on subsequent page loads.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: ConfigTarget with a fromConfig callback is now the supported pattern for wiring text_format elements to config in ConfigFormBase forms

Technical details

The text_format form element's TextFormat::processFormat() builds child elements (value textarea and format selector) by copying most properties from the parent element. #config_target was being copied along with everything else, so ConfigFormBase::doStoreConfigMap() encountered the parent element and the value child both claiming the same config key and threw a LogicException. The fix adds #config_target to the $keys_not_to_copy list inside TextFormat::processFormat(). Because text_format stores a compound value (value and format sub-keys), using #config_target on such an element requires a ConfigTarget instance with a fromConfig callback that extracts only the text portion for the textarea's #default_value, while #format must be set manually from config. The test fixture in form_test demonstrates the intended pattern. A second change in ConfigFormBase::storeConfigKeyToFormElementMap() collects cache tags from all editable configs referenced by the map and applies them to the form render array via CacheableMetadata, ensuring the form cache is invalidated when the underlying configuration changes. This latter change also caused expected cache tag adjustments in several existing browser tests.

Upgrade

To use #config_target on a text_format element, supply a ConfigTarget object with a fromConfig callback and set #format manually:

$form['field'] = [
  '#type' => 'text_format',
  '#title' => $this->t('My field'),
  '#format' => $this->config('mymodule.settings')->get('field.format'),
  '#config_target' => new ConfigTarget(
    'mymodule.settings',
    'field',
    fromConfig: fn (?array $value = NULL): string => $value['value'] ?? '',
  ),
];

The format sub-key is saved automatically by the normal form submission path; no extra handling is needed for it.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 25, 2024
  • Committed: May 9, 2026 (1 year and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • matthieuscarset
  • vinmayiswamy (Material)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • idebr (iO)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

The issue went through two distinct approaches before landing. The first MR (9014, by VinmayiSwamy) added a text_format-specific special case directly inside ConfigFormBase::doStoreConfigMap(). catch redirected the effort, noting that hardcoding a specific element type in the config system was the wrong layer and that the exception exists for a reason: if the usage is valid, the system should accommodate it generically. idebr opened MR 10182 with a different strategy, placing #config_target in TextFormat::processFormat()'s $keys_not_to_copy list instead. alexpott then discovered that the fix did not correctly restore the saved value to the textarea on subsequent page loads, pushed a failing test to demonstrate it, and then committed a follow-up that resolved both the default-value display and the cache invalidation problem (idebr had spotted that the form lacked a cache dependency on the config being edited). The final commit combines all three findings.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579189: Fix return types and baselined errors of core/tests/ Kernel code - round 5
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 69 files, +193/−256 · php +193/−256
  • Diff: 6558643
  • Discussion: #3579189 · 6 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

This is round 5 of a multi-issue effort to add PHP return types and explicit property type declarations to Drupal core's Kernel test code, removing entries from the PHPStan baseline as types are properly declared. The bulk of the changes are test-only. One runtime class also changed: LocaleDefaultConfigStorage now accepts the broader LanguageManagerInterface as its $language_manager constructor argument instead of the narrower ConfigurableLanguageManagerInterface.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: LocaleDefaultConfigStorage::__construct() second parameter type widened from ConfigurableLanguageManagerInterface to LanguageManagerInterface; existing callers require no changes.

Technical details

The primary work is typing the core/tests/ Kernel test layer. KernelTestBase received typed property declarations (?ClassLoader $classLoader, ?ContainerBuilder $container, ?ConfigImporter $configImporter, ?vfsStreamDirectory $vfsRoot, bool $strictConfigSchema, static array $configSchemaCheckerExclusions, etc.) and explicit void/typed return types on its many helper methods (bootEnvironment(), setUpFilesystem(), bootKernel(), config(), getDatabaseConnectionInfo(), enableModules(), disableModules(), render(), and others). AssertContentTrait likewise had all its properties typed and methods annotated with return types. Several individual test classes had their locally-duplicated protected $configImporter declarations removed since KernelTestBase already declares it. Abstract method contracts in test base classes (GenericCacheBackendUnitTestBase, ConfigStorageTestBase) now carry explicit return types.

The one non-test change is in core/modules/locale/src/LocaleDefaultConfigStorage. Its constructor parameter $language_manager was typed as ConfigurableLanguageManagerInterface, but the only method the class calls on it is getStandardLanguageList(), which lives on the parent LanguageManagerInterface. PHPStan flagged a baseline entry because \Drupal::languageManager() declares a return type of LanguageManagerInterface. The fix widens the constructor type to LanguageManagerInterface, which is backward compatible and removes the need for the assertInstanceOf narrowing workaround in the locale test.

Additional minor fixes in test code include initializing variables before loop-entry to satisfy PHPStan ($found = FALSE, $expected_driver = NULL, $new_field_schema_data = []), adding assert(isset($e)) before accessing an exception variable in a finally block, casting $user->passRaw to (string) where a string argument was expected, fixing a PHP error-handler callback to return bool (required by PHP's error handler contract), and replacing some assertSame calls with assertEquals where object identity could not be guaranteed.

Upgrade

If your code constructs LocaleDefaultConfigStorage directly, the second constructor argument has been widened:

// Before: required ConfigurableLanguageManagerInterface
new LocaleDefaultConfigStorage($storage, $configurableLanguageManager, $profile);

// After: accepts any LanguageManagerInterface (existing code is unaffected)
new LocaleDefaultConfigStorage($storage, $languageManager, $profile);

No changes are required for existing callers; the widened type is fully backward compatible.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 13, 2026
  • Committed: May 8, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • xjm (Zoocha)
  • mstrelan
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

During review, xjm flagged that the merge request had grown beyond its stated scope, noting that changes like assertSame to assertEquals conversions and docblock edits were outside the "add return types" mandate. mondrake explained each category of change: the assertInstanceOf type-narrowing pattern was needed to satisfy PHPStan about the ConfigurableLanguageManagerInterface mismatch without touching runtime code. longwave then suggested the cleaner fix of simply widening the LocaleDefaultConfigStorage constructor type to LanguageManagerInterface, since that is the only interface needed there, and mondrake applied it in the final commit.

item.title: #3573879: Mark layout_builder_expose_all_field_blocks obsolete, uninstall it and remove integration code
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +35/−84 · php +34/−83 · yml +1/−1
  • Diff: 9fc95e0
  • Discussion: #3573879 · 7 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The layout_builder_expose_all_field_blocks feature-flag module, previously marked deprecated, is now marked obsolete in Drupal 12. Running database updates will automatically uninstall it on any site where it is still installed. The module allowed all fields across all entity bundles to be exposed as Layout Builder field blocks regardless of whether Layout Builder was enabled on those bundles. That behavior is now gone: field blocks are always scoped only to bundles that have a Layout Builder display configured. Sites relying on field blocks from bundles that do not have Layout Builder enabled may lose those blocks after the update runs.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Database update: New layout_builder_update_12001() uninstalls layout_builder_expose_all_field_blocks automatically on upgrade.

Technical details

The module's lifecycle key in its .info.yml was changed from deprecated to obsolete. The LayoutBuilderExposeAllFieldBlocksHooks class, which contained the sole hook_help implementation, was deleted entirely, as was its generic functional test (GenericTest).

Integration code in LayoutBuilderEntityViewDisplay::save() previously skipped calling \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.block')->clearCachedDefinitions() when the module was installed; that conditional was removed and the cache invalidation now runs unconditionally on every save.

In FieldBlockDeriver::getDerivativeDefinitions() and getFieldMap(), the $expose_all_fields branch that bypassed bundle-level filtering when the module was present was removed. Both methods now always filter entity types and bundles to only those with configured Layout Builder displays.

A new update hook, layout_builder_update_12001(), calls module_installer->uninstall(['layout_builder_expose_all_field_blocks']) if the module is still installed, ensuring it is removed on upgrade. Tests that previously relied on the module to expose user entity field blocks were updated to instead explicitly enable Layout Builder on the user entity's default display in their setUp().

Upgrade

Sites running layout_builder_expose_all_field_blocks will have it uninstalled automatically by the update hook. No manual action is required to trigger uninstallation. However, you should audit any Layout Builder displays that used field blocks from entity bundles that do not themselves have Layout Builder enabled: those field blocks will no longer be available after the module is uninstalled. To preserve access to such fields, enable Layout Builder on the relevant bundle's display before running updates.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 16, 2026
  • Committed: May 8, 2026 (2 months later)

Key contributors

  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • ishani patel
  • danielveza (PreviousNext)
  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

catch pointed out early in the thread that the module can only be marked obsolete in 12.x, not 11.x, because the update hook guaranteeing uninstallation must have run before the module can be removed. danielveza asked whether the integration code inside LayoutBuilderEntityViewDisplay::save and FieldBlockDeriver should also be removed as part of this issue; catch confirmed that it should and bumped the issue to Major as a 12.0.0 beta blocker. gábor hojtsy then took over the implementation, rewrote the patch to remove the integration code, and marked it ready for review.

item.title: #2954725: AccountInterface::getLastAccessedTime() implementors return incorrect data type
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +35/−4 · php +35/−4
  • Diff: 5af6f43
  • Discussion: #2954725 · 12 contributors · 53 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

AccountInterface::getLastAccessedTime() is documented to return an int, but both User and UserSession implementations were returning string values sourced directly from database columns. This caused silent failures in strict comparisons (using ===) and was inconsistent with the method's documented contract. The return values in both implementations are now cast to int.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause is that PHP database drivers return column values as strings by default. User::getLastAccessedTime() called $this->getFieldValue('access', 'value') and UserSession::getLastAccessedTime() returned $this->access directly, neither applying any cast.

The fix adds (int) casts in both User::getLastAccessedTime() (core/modules/user/src/Entity/User.php) and UserSession::getLastAccessedTime() (core/lib/Drupal/Core/Session/UserSession.php). The @var docblock on UserSession::$access is corrected from string to int. AccountProxy::getLastAccessedTime() is unaffected because it delegates to the underlying account object, so it picks up the fix through the cast on those implementations.

The migrate kernel test MigrateUserTest::testUser() was updated to cast $source->access before the assertSame() call, since the migration source record still carries a string but getLastAccessedTime() now guarantees an int. New testGetLastAccessedTime() kernel tests were added to UserEntityTest and UserSessionTest to pin the int-return contract going forward.

Upgrade

If you were working around this bug by casting the return value yourself, those casts can now be removed:

// Before (workaround)
$lastAccess = (int) $user->getLastAccessedTime();

// After (no cast needed)
$lastAccess = $user->getLastAccessedTime();

// Strict comparisons now work correctly
if ($user->getLastAccessedTime() === 0) { /* user never accessed */ }

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 20, 2018
  • First commit: April 20, 2026 (8 years later)
  • Last commit: May 8, 2026 (17 days and 4 commits later)

Key contributors

  • dpi (PreviousNext)
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • manuel.adan (Arcovía Digital)
  • dcam
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • godotislate

item.title: #3583594: Add getUrl() and (renamed) xpath() helpers to HttpKernelUiHelperTrait
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +34/−0 · php +34
  • Diff: 326c357
  • Discussion: #3583594 · 5 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Two helper methods have been added to HttpKernelUiHelperTrait (\Drupal\Tests) to reduce friction when converting Browser tests to Kernel tests. getNodeElementsByXpath() performs an XPath search against the current page and returns Mink NodeElement objects, matching the behaviour of BrowserTestBase::xpath(). getUrl() returns the current URL from the Mink session. Both methods are now available in any Kernel test class that uses this trait, including KernelTestBase subclasses.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two methods added to HttpKernelUiHelperTrait: getNodeElementsByXpath(string $xpath, array $arguments = []): array returning \Behat\Mink\Element\NodeElement[], and getUrl(): string returning the current Mink session URL.

Technical details

Kernel tests already include AssertContentTrait::xpath() (via KernelTestBase), which returns \SimpleXMLElement[]|false. BrowserTestBase::xpath() returns \Behat\Mink\Element\NodeElement[]. Adding a second xpath() directly to HttpKernelUiHelperTrait causes a fatal PHP trait method collision in KernelTestBase. To avoid this, the Mink-based XPath helper is added under the distinct name getNodeElementsByXpath(string $xpath, array $arguments = []): array. It delegates to $this->assertSession()->buildXPathQuery() for argument substitution and then calls $this->getSession()->getPage()->findAll('xpath', $xpath), exactly mirroring the BrowserTestBase implementation. getUrl() simply wraps $this->getSession()->getCurrentUrl(), matching the contract of UiHelperTrait::getUrl(). A follow-on issue (#3585774) is filed to standardise and disambiguate the names of all xpath() variants across the test suite.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 8, 2026
  • Committed: May 8, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • dww (3281d Consulting)
  • mstrelan
  • joachim
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)

Collaboration

joachim flagged that directly porting xpath() would be hazardous because the two existing variants return incompatible types, and proposed naming the new method something unambiguous like getNodeElementByXpath(). That observation drove the decision to add the method under the distinctive name getNodeElementsByXpath() rather than as a plain copy of xpath(), and to file #3585774 as a follow-on cleanup.

item.title: #3563639: Add getAllKeys() method to KeyValueStoreInterface
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 9 files, +71/−4 · php +71/−4
  • Diff: 3971cf1
  • Discussion: #3563639 · 7 contributors · 32 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Drupal's key-value store API gains a new getAllKeys() method on KeyValueStoreInterface. Developers working with large key-value collections can now retrieve only the collection's keys without pulling all values into memory, making chunked or batch-processing workflows practical where calling getAll() would previously cause memory exhaustion.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New getAllKeys(): iterable method on KeyValueStoreInterface returns only collection keys without loading values.

Technical details

The new getAllKeys(): iterable method is declared on KeyValueStoreInterface and implemented in four concrete classes. DatabaseStorage issues a lightweight SELECT [name] query against the key-value table, returning only key names. DatabaseStorageExpirable does the same but adds the AND [expire] > :now clause so only non-expired keys are returned. A null storage implementation returns an empty array. The abstract StorageBase base class receives a default implementation via array_keys($this->getAll()), which means any contrib or custom class that extends StorageBase automatically satisfies the interface without requiring code changes. Adding a method to an interface is permitted under Drupal's BC policy when a base class covers the default; catch requested the StorageBase implementation specifically for this compatibility reason. EntitySchemaTest was updated to use getAllKeys() in place of getAll() followed by array_keys(), demonstrating the intended usage. Related issue #3576212 (unbuffered MySQL queries) was noted as a complementary approach to the same memory-pressure problem, though that is considered far from a general recommendation.

Upgrade

Any class that directly implements KeyValueStoreInterface (without extending StorageBase) must add the new method:

public function getAllKeys(): iterable {
    // Return an iterable of key strings for this collection.
    return array_keys($this->getAll());
}

Classes that extend StorageBase inherit this default implementation and require no changes.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: December 17, 2025
  • Committed: May 8, 2026 (4 months and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • plopesc (Lullabot)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • ghost of drupal past
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

During review, "ghost of drupal past" raised two concerns: return types on new interface methods are now required, and the return type should allow lazy iteration rather than forcing a full array. plopesc resolved both points by adopting iterable as the return type, which is equivalent to array|\Traversable and avoids PHP 8.2 iterable-compatibility issues. Later, catch identified that to stay within Drupal's interface BC policy the base class StorageBase must carry a default implementation, not only the leaf classes. plopesc added the StorageBase implementation, which catch confirmed resolved the compatibility concern.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588558: Test failures on 11.x
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +1/−1 · php +1/−1
  • Diff: 78659fb
  • Discussion: #3588558 · 3 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The node access rebuild confirmation form (RebuildPermissionsForm) declared its injected service property with readonly, which conflicted with DependencySerializationTrait. Whenever the form was serialized and then deserialized (for example, when form state is stored in a session), the trait's __wakeup() method tried to re-initialize the property from the container and PHP threw an error because readonly properties cannot be assigned outside the constructor. Removing readonly from that property fixes the crash.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The problem originates from #3533299 (Deprecate node access rebuild functions), which introduced RebuildPermissionsForm with a constructor-promoted protected readonly NodeAccessRebuild $nodeAccessRebuild property. RebuildPermissionsForm extends ConfirmFormBase, which uses DependencySerializationTrait. That trait's __wakeup() loops over an object's properties and re-fetches any that match known service IDs from the container, writing them back by name. PHP does not allow assignment to a readonly property after construction, so __wakeup() raises a fatal error on deserialization.

The fix in MR !15700 is a single-character change in core/modules/node/src/Form/RebuildPermissionsForm.php: the readonly modifier is dropped from the constructor-promoted property, leaving it protected NodeAccessRebuild $nodeAccessRebuild. During review, the question arose of whether readonly should also be removed from the main branch. catch concluded it was acceptable to keep it on main because properties on form controllers are @internal, so subclass compatibility is not a concern for the public API surface.

The two mysqli-related update-test failures mentioned in the issue body (from #3581109 and #3184242) were tracked separately in #3588702 and are not part of this merged diff.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 5, 2026
  • Committed: May 8, 2026 (2 days later)

Key contributors

  • godotislate
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

During review, godotislate raised a subtle forward-compatibility concern: if main kept the readonly modifier, a site upgrading from 11.4 to 12.0 with a subclass that redeclared the property without readonly could hit a PHP error. catch replied that form controller properties are @internal by convention, so there was no compatibility obligation, and readonly could stay on main. The discussion was brief and the fix proceeded as readonly-removal on 11.x only.

item.title: #3588385: Add a transaction around the database key value store setMultiple()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +14/−0 · php +14
  • Diff: d2f42da
  • Discussion: #3588385 · 3 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The database-backed key-value store now writes all entries from a setMultiple() call inside a single database transaction. Previously, each key was written individually with no transactional protection, so a failure partway through could leave only some keys stored. The change makes bulk writes atomic: either all keys are committed or none are, which is safer during concurrent requests or unexpected errors.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The base class KeyValueStoreBase::setMultiple() loops over its input and calls $this->set() for each key. KeyValueDatabaseStore did not override this, so there was no transaction wrapping the individual set() calls. The fix adds a setMultiple() override in KeyValueDatabaseStore that opens a transaction via $this->connection->startTransaction(), delegates to parent::setMultiple($data), and calls $transaction->commitOrRelease() on success or $transaction->rollBack() on any exception.

A follow-up in #3586760 is exploring replacing this pattern with a single multi-row UPSERT query, which would make the transaction unnecessary by making the entire operation a single SQL statement.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 5, 2026
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (2 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • mondrake
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3578196: Remove media and user links from the Content menu
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +4/−40 · php +4/−40
  • Diff: 56d0ae2
  • Discussion: #3578196 · 8 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 11

Summary

The Navigation module's "Content" menu (labeled "Create" in the sidebar) previously showed hard-coded sub-items for the "Document" and "Image" media types and a "User" creation link in addition to all node types. User testing found this confusing: visitors expected only content-creation shortcuts like pages and blog posts under "Create," and the User entry was easily mistaken for a user-profile type. This change removes the media and user sub-items so the Content menu shows only node type links by default. Sites that relied on those quick-create links for Document, Image, or User should re-add them manually as menu items.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: Navigation module's Content (Create) menu no longer shows Document, Image media type links or the User creation link by default; only node types appear.

Technical details

The fix is in NavigationContentLinks::addMenuLinks(). The call to $this->addCreateEntityLinks('media_type', 'entity.media.add_form', $links, ['document', 'image']), which hard-coded the allowed media-type IDs, is removed entirely. The block that added the bundleless navigation.create.user link pointing to user.admin_create with a weight of 100 is also removed. Only the addCreateEntityLinks('node_type', 'entity.node.add_form', $links) call remains.

The kernel test NavigationContentLinksTest drops MediaTypeCreationTrait and UserCreationTrait, removes the media-type setup steps, and removes the corresponding assertions for navigation.create.user, navigation.content.media_type.document, navigation.content.media_type.image, and the negative assertion for navigation.content.media_type.special. The performance test's expected QueryCount drops from 264 to 263, reflecting one fewer query without the User link resolution.

Upgrade

Sites that want to restore the removed links can add them back manually as Navigation menu items. A change record was created at https://www.drupal.org/node/3581445 with instructions for doing so.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 10, 2026
  • First commit: March 25, 2026 (15 days later)
  • Last commit: May 7, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • pameeela (Technocrat)
  • emma horrell (The University of Edinburgh)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)
  • godotislate
  • benjifisher (Harvard Web Publishing)

Collaboration

emma horrell reported direct user-testing evidence that drove the decision: participants using the "Create" menu expected only page and post types, found the "User" item confusing because it resembled a profile type, and expected media to be managed through the dedicated Media section of the sidebar, not through "Create." This grounded the consensus that removing the hard-coded entries was the right call rather than a debatable preference.

item.title: #2258355: Deprecate hide() and show()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +65/−30 · php +65/−30
  • Diff: afe536a
  • Discussion: #2258355 · 25 contributors · 131 comments
  • Followers: 25

Summary

The global procedural functions hide() and show() defined in core/includes/common.inc are deprecated as of Drupal 10.1.0 and will be removed in Drupal 11.0.0. Code that calls these functions will trigger a deprecation notice. The replacement is to set the render array property directly: use $element['#printed'] = TRUE in place of hide($element), and $element['#printed'] = FALSE in place of show($element). The one core usage of these functions, in the file field preprocess function, has been updated to use the direct property assignment. The conceptual documentation about how the #printed flag controls rendering traversal has been moved from the individual function docblocks into RendererInterface::render(), where it more accurately belongs.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Global hide() and show() functions deprecated in drupal:10.1.0 and removed in drupal:11.0.0; replace with direct $element['#printed'] = TRUE/FALSE assignments.

Technical details

Both hide() and show() in core/includes/common.inc simply flip the #printed boolean property on a render array element. The #printed flag acts as a semaphore: when TRUE, the renderer skips that element and its children on subsequent render passes. Because the functions are nothing more than thin wrappers around a one-line property assignment, the deprecation has no replacement function; callers are directed to inline the assignment. The @trigger_error() call in each function points to change record https://www.drupal.org/node/3261271.

The docblock explaining the #printed caching and traversal semantics (the "first time render() is called" explanation) was stripped from the two deprecated functions and added to RendererInterface::render() in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Render/RendererInterface.php, since that is where the behavior is actually implemented.

The sole core callsite, template_preprocess_file_widget_multiple() in core/modules/file/file.field.inc, was updated to use $widget[$sub_key]['#printed'] = TRUE and $widget['display']['#printed'] = TRUE in place of hide(), and $operations_elements[$key]['#printed'] = FALSE in place of show(). A new unit test class ProceduralApiDeprecationTest in core/tests/Drupal/Tests/Core/Render/Element/ verifies both deprecation messages fire and that the functions still return the expected array shapes during the transition window.

Upgrade

Replace every call to the global hide() and show() functions with a direct property assignment:

// Before
hide($element['some_child']);
show($element['some_child']);

// After
$element['some_child']['#printed'] = TRUE;
$element['some_child']['#printed'] = FALSE;

Note: if you are hiding a form element to remove it from output, using $element['#access'] = FALSE is often more appropriate, as it also prevents form submission of that element.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 5, 2014
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (12 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcingy
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • Arnion (Skilld)
  • andypost (Skilld)
  • MerryHamster (Skilld)
  • karishmaamin (Specbee)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • nnevill (DevBranch)
  • voleger (Drupal Ukraine Community)
  • nod_ (Acquia)
  • bhanu951
  • spokje
  • xjm
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • vladimiraus (Tomato Elephant Studio)
  • bnjmnm (Acquia)

Collaboration

longwave raised a substantive design question late in the review: the original plan was to move hide() and show() to static methods on \Drupal\Core\Render\Element, but longwave noted that with Twig and its without() filter now covering the themer use case, and most contrib usage actually being form-element hiding that should use #access instead, there was no strong reason to keep the methods as public API at all. andypost picked up the point, updated the MR to deprecate without replacement, and moved the #printed traversal documentation to RendererInterface::render(). The shift from "move to a class" to "deprecate with no replacement" emerged directly from this exchange.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3573954: Move common code across Drupal base test suite classes to DrupalTestCaseTrait
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 12 files, +75/−87 · php +75/−87
  • Diff: fe682be
  • Discussion: #3573954 · 5 contributors · 24 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Three symbols used in custom and contrib test classes are deprecated in Drupal 12 and will be removed in Drupal 13: TestRequirementsTrait, its getDrupalRoot() method, and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot(). The underlying functionality (resolving the Drupal root path and registering the debug dump handler) has been consolidated into DrupalTestCaseTrait, which all Drupal base test classes already use. Developers whose test code references any of the deprecated symbols will start seeing deprecation notices and must update before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: TestRequirementsTrait, TestRequirementsTrait::getDrupalRoot(), and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot() deprecated in drupal:12.0.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; use DrupalTestCaseTrait and access $this->root directly.

Technical details

The consolidation was motivated by three identical copies of setDebugDumpHandler() (a #[BeforeClass] hook registering the VarDumper CLI handler) and two copies of getDrupalRoot() living independently in KernelTestBase, BrowserTestBase, and UnitTestCase. Both methods, plus the $root property previously declared redundantly in those classes and in TestSetupTrait, are now defined once in DrupalTestCaseTrait.

The $root property in DrupalTestCaseTrait is declared with a PHP 8.4 property hook for lazy initialization: the get hook calls static::getDrupalRoot() on first access and caches the result, so explicit setUp() assignments in subclasses are no longer needed. Because property hooks require PHP 8.4, this change targets the main branch (Drupal 12) only. A phpcs:ignoreFile directive was added to DrupalTestCaseTrait temporarily because PHPCS does not yet parse hooked property syntax.

TestRequirementsTrait is deprecated at the file level via a top-of-file @trigger_error, its getDrupalRoot() method carries an individual deprecation notice, and BrowserTestBase::setUpAppRoot() is deprecated with a notice to access $this->root directly. The change record is at https://www.drupal.org/node/3574112.

Upgrade

If your test class uses TestRequirementsTrait, remove it. All Drupal base test classes (UnitTestCase, KernelTestBase, BrowserTestBase) already pull the same functionality from DrupalTestCaseTrait.

// Before
use Drupal\Tests\TestRequirementsTrait;

class MyTest extends UnitTestCase {
  use TestRequirementsTrait;
  ...
}

// After — no use statement needed; functionality comes from UnitTestCase
class MyTest extends UnitTestCase {
  ...
}

If your test code calls static::getDrupalRoot(), access $this->root instead:

// Before
$root = static::getDrupalRoot();

// After
$root = $this->root;

If your test class overrides or calls setUpAppRoot(), remove the call:

// Before (in setUp())
$this->setUpAppRoot();

// After — remove the call; $this->root is initialized lazily via the property hook

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 17, 2026
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (2 months later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dcam
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

item.title: #3066751: Add resolvable_uri property to LinkItem for APIs
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 8 files, +196/−10 · php +196/−10
  • Diff: ff86b9f
  • Discussion: #3066751 · 27 contributors · 106 comments
  • Followers: 48

Summary

Link fields exposed through JSON:API and REST previously returned only the internal URI format (such as entity:node/5 or internal:/node/add), which decoupled front-end applications cannot use directly to render links. A new computed property, resolvable_uri, is now included in link field output alongside the existing uri, title, and options properties. It contains the processed, routable URL: relative paths for internal links (for example /node/5) and full absolute URLs for external links. The property can also be used to set a link field item's value in PHP, with the internal uri and options being derived automatically.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New computed resolvable_uri property on LinkItem (and new LinkResolvableUriComputed DataType plugin) that returns the processed, routable URL for a link field item; appears in JSON:API and REST output and can be written to in PHP.

Technical details

A new DataType plugin, LinkResolvableUriComputed (plugin id resolvable_uri), is registered in core/modules/link/src/Plugin/DataType/LinkResolvableUriComputed.php. It extends Uri and implements CacheableDependencyInterface. Its getValue() method calls $item->getUrl()->toString(TRUE) on the parent LinkItem and caches the resulting GeneratedUrl; the getCacheTags(), getCacheContexts(), and getCacheMaxAge() methods delegate to that same object so cacheability metadata is correctly propagated.

LinkItem::propertyDefinitions() registers resolvable_uri as a computed, non-internal, read-only property using this data type. LinkItem::onChange() is added to keep the stored properties in sync when resolvable_uri is written directly: it parses the incoming URL, prepends the internal: scheme when the path contains no scheme marker, and calls writePropertyValue() for uri and optionally options. LinkItem::setValue() is updated to call onChange('resolvable_uri', FALSE) when the value array contains a resolvable_uri key but no uri key, enabling the field to be set entirely through the new property (for example $entity->field_link = ['resolvable_uri' => '/node/add']).

The property name went through two earlier iterations. url was blocked because Twig resolves field.0.url as a call to LinkItem::getUrl(), shadowing the method and silently returning NULL for any chained access like field.0.url.routename. The name was changed to full_url in community patches that were widely deployed, then finally to resolvable_uri in the MR that landed.

Upgrade

No action is required for sites that have not applied earlier community patches. Sites or front-end applications that relied on the url or full_url property from unofficial patches must rename all references to resolvable_uri.

// Old (patch-era, never in a Drupal core release):
$entity->field_link->url
$entity->field_link->full_url

// New (Drupal core, 11.x / main):
$entity->field_link->resolvable_uri

// Setting a link field item via the new property:
$entity->field_link->resolvable_uri = 'https://www.drupal.org?test_param=value#main-content';

// Or in an array assignment (uri is derived automatically):
$entity->field_link = ['resolvable_uri' => '/node/add'];

JSON:API and REST responses will now include resolvable_uri in every link field item. Any client code that deserializes or validates link field shapes may need to be updated to accept the new key.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2019
  • Committed: May 7, 2026 (6 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • justafish (Lullabot)
  • jibran (PreviousNext)
  • lauriii (Acquia)
  • duozersk (Five Jars)
  • jonnyeom
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • vsujeetkumar (Material)
  • andregp (CI&T)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • wim leers (Acquia)
  • greg__
  • kristen pol (Itty Bitty Byte)
  • nitesh sethia (Digital Polygon)
  • dcam
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

The property name produced a significant design dispute. An early reviewer (greg__) reported in comment #22 that the original name url silently broke Twig templates: field.0.url.routename resolves in Twig to $item->getUrl()->getRouteName(), so the new computed property shadowed the existing method and returned NULL for any chained access. jibran and lauriii confirmed the conflict. alexpott explicitly blocked commitment in comment #45, pointing to contrib templates already using the pattern and stating "This looks disruptive in its current form." The issue sat at RTBC for over a month before that review.

The name was changed to full_url and community patches under that name were widely deployed for years. When dcam reopened the MR in 2025 targeting 11.x, he renamed the property to resolvable_uri, citing semantic clarity. dgroene objected in comment #98 that yet another rename was painful for sites already running full_url in production. Committer longwave acknowledged the history but agreed that full_url was not sufficiently descriptive and committed under the resolvable_uri name, noting minor typing issues would be cleaned up separately rather than holding the issue any longer.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579661: Call to a member function getPath() on null in EntityRouteHelper->isContentEntityRoute()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +11/−3 · php +11/−3
  • Diff: c683664
  • Discussion: #3579661 · 6 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Sites with the Navigation module enabled crashed with a fatal error when any non-existent URL was visited. The error surfaced during page rendering while the Navigation module tried to determine whether the current route is a content-entity route, and the route object was null for 404 pages. This fix prevents the crash so 404 pages render normally. The patch was committed to main, 11.x, and 11.3.x.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

EntityRouteHelper::isContentEntityRoute() called $this->routeMatch->getRouteObject()->getPath() without first checking whether getRouteObject() returns a non-null value. On a 404 route the route object is null, causing the fatal Call to a member function getPath() on null. The call chain was: TopBar::preRenderTopBar -> PageActions::build -> NavigationRenderer::hasLocalTasks -> NavigationRenderer::getLocalTasks -> EntityRouteHelper::isContentEntityRoute.

The fix introduces an early if (!$route) { return FALSE; } guard, consistent with the pattern already applied to getContentEntityFromRoute() in the same class (see #3565886, which fixed the same class for a related method but missed isContentEntityRoute()). A kernel test assertion for isContentEntityRoute() returning FALSE on a non-existent route was added to the existing NavigationEntityRouteHelperTest.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 17, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • kevinvb
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • sivaji_ganesh_jojodae (Material)
  • penyaskito (Acquia)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

penyaskito noted they had encountered the same error while working on Drupal CMS and adjusted the patch: they renamed the kernel test to cover both methods and changed the fix to match the approach already used in getContentEntityFromRoute(), making the two methods consistent. smustgrave verified the test reproduced the error and confirmed LGTM.

item.title: #3570634: Remove fallback classloader and related code in AttributeClassDiscovery
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +18/−191 · php +18/−191
  • Diff: 15d0117
  • Discussion: #3570634 · 3 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The temporary workaround that let plugin attribute discovery handle missing traits without a fatal error has been removed from AttributeClassDiscovery. The workaround was introduced in #3502913 to paper over a PHP limitation where loading a class that uses a non-existent trait caused an unrecoverable fatal error. PHP 8.5 fixes this by making the same situation throw a catchable \Error, so the workaround is no longer needed. The practical effect for most sites is nothing: plugins whose classes depend on uninstalled modules continue to be silently skipped during discovery, just through simpler code.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Requirements: Relies on PHP 8.5 behavior where loading a class with a missing trait throws a catchable \Error; this removal is only safe because Drupal main now targets PHP 8.5.

Technical details

The removed workaround registered MissingClassDetectionClassLoader as an spl_autoload handler during every getDefinitions() call. When PHP tried to load a class with a missing trait, this handler fired last, recorded the missing trait in $missingTraits, and aliased it to StubTrait so reflection could proceed. AttributeClassDiscovery tracked affected classes in a static $skipClasses array and re-checked them on subsequent discovery passes, comparing the missing trait's two-level namespace against registered plugin namespaces to decide whether to allow or skip the class. Plugins with missing traits were also excluded from the file cache so they would be re-scanned on every request.

With PHP 8.5, class_exists() on a class that uses a non-existent trait throws a catchable \Error. The diff deletes MissingClassDetectionClassLoader and StubTrait entirely, removes the spl_autoload_register / spl_autoload_unregister calls, drops the $skipClasses static property and all associated re-check logic, and reduces the error-handling block inside getDefinitions() to a bare catch (\Error) { continue; }. File cache writes for successfully resolved plugins are now unconditional.

The one visible behavioral difference, captured in the updated AttributeClassDiscoveryCachedTest::testGetDefinitionsMissingTrait(), is that if discovery runs twice in a single request and the missing trait's module is installed between the two passes, the plugin is no longer re-discovered in the second pass. Previously the stub-alias meant PHP would not throw again, so the class appeared valid. Now PHP 8.5 throws an \Error on every class_exists() call for that class until the next full discovery cycle. The thread also considered restoring a preg_match('/(Class|Interface|Trait) .* not found$/', $e->getMessage()) guard to prevent silently swallowing unexpected \Error exceptions, but contributors decided against it as fragile given how unlikely such exceptions are in practice; the tracking issue #3520811 is expected to be closed as won't-do.

Upgrade

Both removed classes were @internal and not part of Drupal's public API. If you referenced them despite that marker, remove those references:

// Remove these imports and any usage:
use Drupal\Component\Discovery\MissingClassDetectionClassLoader;
use Drupal\Component\Discovery\StubTrait;

If your code installs a module and then calls getDefinitions() again in the same request expecting to find a previously-missing-trait plugin, that plugin will no longer appear until the next request's discovery cycle. Move such code to a fresh request or clear the plugin cache after module installation.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 30, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (3 months later)

Collaboration

catch, who wrote the original MissingClassDetectionClassLoader workaround in #3502913, remarked: "Was pleased with myself when I got this working in the original issue, but even more pleased to get rid of it as soon as humanly possible," and at merge time called it "a horrible hack." godotislate raised a substantive concern: removing the classloader without restoring the earlier preg_match guard means any unexpected \Error from class_exists() would be silently swallowed. catch and godotislate weighed this against the brittleness of matching on exception message strings and concluded the risk was acceptable, pointing to #3520811 as the place to track that question.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3587182: Update CKEditor 5 to 48.0.1
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +31/−31 · yml +26/−26 · php +2/−2 · js +2/−2 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 30521cd
  • Discussion: #3587182 · 6 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

CKEditor 5 has been updated from 47.6.0 to 48.0.1 in Drupal core's main branch (Drupal 12 only; Drupal 11 stays on the CKEditor 5 v47 LTS line per an agreement with CKSource). The new version resolves long-standing HTML table problems: the editor no longer collapses multiple <tbody> sections or strips <thead>, which had impeded screen-reader accessibility. Support for <tfoot> and correct scope attributes on header cells is also included. This upgrade was blocked until the switch away from the DLL installation format landed in #3527914.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The version strings in core/assets/vendor/ckeditor5/ckeditor5.libraries.yml were bumped from 47.6.0 to 48.0.1 across every sub-library entry (the per-plugin libraries such as ckeditor5.table remain present but are already deprecated for removal in 13.0.0). The npm devDependency in core/package.json was updated from the ~47.6.0 range to ^48.0.1. CKEditor 5 v48 dropped the DLL build format entirely, so #3527914 (switching to the UMD installation method) was a hard prerequisite for this upgrade.

The core/scripts/js/jsdoc-generate.js script was also fixed for compatibility with v48's distribution layout: the glob was changed from ./ckeditor5*/src/**/*.+(js|jsdoc) to ./ckeditor5*/dist/**/*.d.ts (TypeScript declaration files), and the export-matching regex was extended to recognise declare, interface, const, type, and enum in addition to class and function. This was prompted by a review comment from godotislate noting the script produced an empty output against the v48 package structure; fixing it also allowed closing #3551053 as "won't fix".

The AssetAggregationAcrossPagesTest byte thresholds were updated to reflect the slightly smaller bundle produced by v48: ScriptBytes dropped from 3,222,537 to 3,206,361 and StylesheetBytes from 1,386,351 to 1,286,838.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 27, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (8 days later)

Key contributors

  • joseph.olstad (Entreprise 7pro.ca Inc)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • godotislate
  • andrew.wang (Ontario Digital Service)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

When the issue was filed, longwave immediately flagged that CKEditor 5 v48 had dropped DLL support, making the upgrade impossible until #3527914 (the UMD installation-method switch) landed, and noted that Drupal 11 would deliberately stay on the v47 LTS line. Once that prerequisite merged, longwave opened the MR. During review godotislate observed that the jsdoc generation script was silently producing empty output against v48's changed package layout; longwave identified that v48 ships TypeScript .d.ts files instead of JS source files, updated the glob and regex accordingly, and closed the separate tracking issue #3551053 as no longer needed. After an initial RTBC, a merge conflict caused the asset-byte test to fail; godotislate rebased, updated the byte thresholds, and moved the issue back to RTBC for final commit.
